channel.title: Science Fiction on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Science Fiction on Alien Truth Social

item.title: UFO religion
item.description:
A UFO religion, also known as a UFO cult, is a religion where belief in extraterrestrial entities and communication with them is central. These religions often posit that aliens are interested in humanity’s welfare and can help solve Earth’s problems with their advanced technology and spirituality. Their origins are linked to science fiction, ufology, and UFO sighting narratives. Some historians consider the Aetherius Society or the “I AM” Activity to be the first UFO religions.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_religion

item.title: UFO (2018 film)
item.description:
UFO (2018) is a science fiction film written and directed by Ryan Eslinger that follows a mathematics student, played by Alex Sharp, as he attempts to prove the reality of extraterrestrial encounters. Assisted by a professor portrayed by Gillian Anderson, the protagonist decodes complex signals to challenge official government cover-ups regarding a UAP sighting. The film offers a grounded, math-centric take on the UFO genre, though critical reception suggests it serves primarily as a modest nod to classic alien-encounter cinema. Sony Pictures released the project directly to video on September 4, 2018.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%282018_film%29

item.title: The Extraterrestrial UFO Hypothesis (ETH)
item.description:
The extraterrestrial hypothesis (ETH) proposes that some unidentified flying objects (UFOs) are physical spacecraft piloted by intelligent extraterrestrial beings. While historical speculation about alien visitors dates back centuries, the modern ETH gained traction in the late 1940s and 1950s, influenced by early science fiction and widespread UFO sightings. Despite some historical government and private studies suggesting extraterrestrial origins, the scientific community largely attributes UFO reports to misinterpretations, hoaxes, or common phenomena, with no definitive physical evidence of alien visitation found.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extraterrestrial_UFO_hypothesis

item.title: UFO
item.description:
The 2018 American science fiction film UFO stars Gillian Anderson and Alex Sharp. It follows a mathematics student who attempts to prove the existence of extraterrestrials after a UFO sighting causes electromagnetic interference. With the help of his professor, he deciphers a message containing the fine-structure constant and coordinates. The film was released directly to video on September 4, 2018.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%282018_film%29

item.title: Xenoarchaeology
item.description:
Xenoarchaeology is a hypothetical field of study concerned with the material remains of extraterrestrial cultures, primarily existing in science fiction. It is a branch of xenology that aims to reconstruct and interpret past alien civilizations. The field is not currently practiced by mainstream archaeologists due to a lack of actual material to study. Xenoarchaeology is also referred to as astroarchaeology or exoarchaeology and encompasses related concepts like SETI and SETA.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xenoarchaeology

item.title: Cultural Tracking in UFO Reports
item.description:
In ufology, cultural tracking describes how UFO reports evolve to mirror contemporary cultural changes and technological advancements. Historically, reported UFOs have reflected the era’s technology, from mysterious airships in the 1890s to flying saucers resembling current aerospace designs. This phenomenon also influences UFO narratives by incorporating elements from prevalent science fiction, such as car engine stoppages or specific alien appearances. Cultural tracking challenges the extraterrestrial hypothesis, suggesting instead a psychosocial explanation where UFOs and their occupants are imaginary, though some proponents of the interdimensional hypothesis believe advanced beings deliberately alter their appearance.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cultural_tracking
